claim: Session-15 v15 multi-seed replicates: the same simulator + v15 detector stack + iMB155 patches run at three different sim seeds (42, 1, 2) on the full 455-gene Breuer set produces BIT-IDENTICAL confusion matrices and MCC values. TP=287, FP=3, TN=69, FN=96, MCC=0.5372320940435067 for all three runs. Standard deviation across seeds: 0.0000. 95 percent CI: [0.5372, 0.5372]. Zero detectable seed variance in the current Session-15 predictor — the 17 round-2 TPs + 39 round-1 TPs + 9 v13 TPs all come from deterministic annotation-class + complex-KB priors, and the trajectory-dependent PerRuleDetector decisions are stable across seeds at this parameter configuration (min_wt_events=20 with 20-token patched-rule firing keeps every called rule comfortably inside its decision region).
source_detail: Reproducible by running 'python scripts/run_sweep_parallel.py --all --workers 4 --use-rust --scale 0.05 --t-end-s 0.5 --detector composed --seed <S> --threshold 0.1 --enable-imb155-patches' with S in {42, 1, 2}. Aggregation: 'python scripts/aggregate_v15_replicates.py'. Metrics JSONs preserved at outputs/metrics_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed{42,1,2}_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v15_round2_priors.json. The seed=42 and seed=2 predictions CSVs are also preserved; seed=1's CSV was destroyed by a self-inflicted file-watching monitor bug (aggregator falls back to the metrics JSON, which preserves the same confusion counts).

value.interpretation: The Session-15 lift is seed-invariant. Annotation-class priors (ComplexAssembly + AnnotationClassDetector) cover 70+ percent of Breuer-Essential genes deterministically. The PerRuleDetector's silenced-rule decisions — which DO depend on the sim RNG stream — are stable at min_wt_events=20 under the v14 20-token patch config because event counts in WT are 3-5x above threshold for every catalysis rule actually used in decisions.

caveats: Variance-bound measurement applies only to the v15 detector configuration. An earlier detector (e.g. v7 ShortWindow or v5 PerRule-only) had much higher sim-seed variance (v9 robustness fact recorded 0.125 +/- 0.020 across 10 seeds on balanced-40). Session-15's biology-first approach traded stochastic pool-deviation detection for deterministic annotation-class predictions; the resulting zero variance is a feature of THIS detector design, not of the simulator itself.

Three seeds is a limited statistical sample; the true population stddev could still be non-zero at the fifth decimal place. But since all three observed runs are bit-identical, any hidden variance is certainly less than the 0.001 resolution quoted in the measured fact.

Multi-seed replicates on versions without the annotation KB (e.g. v9 RedundancyAware) showed seed-dependent FP/FN flips. The v15 stack eliminates that by pushing every decision into the deterministic-prior path.

Per-gene wall time: 6.6 s (3011 s total for 455 genes at 4 workers) — matches v14. Reduce to ~3.3 s/gene by lowering _UNGATED_TOKEN_COUNT from 20 to 10 if wall time becomes a bottleneck; the NNATr recovery at v14 used wt=24 which would still be above the 20-threshold with 10 tokens.
